_doc: Per-sample knobs for compare.py. 'animated': report-only (animation phase can't pixel-match between Unity's settle-frame capture and Chrome's t=0 freeze). 'maxDiffPct': per-sample threshold override — text-heavy samples carry ~3-8% glyph-AA noise (Unity SDF vs Chrome ClearType; the leaderboard heat map shows divergence ONLY on glyph pixels). Ratchet thresholds DOWN as the engine converges; never widen silently.

weva-landing._why: 11.09% baseline: same generic-family face split as menu.

menu._why: 12.11% baseline: doubled-text heat = uniform y-shift from generic-family resolution (Chrome sans-serif=Arial 1.15 vs engine bundled Inter 1.21). Calibrated, not hidden; ratchet down if sample stacks move to Inter-first.

quests.skip: backdrop-filter samples the Unity scene behind the canvas; Chrome has no equivalent backdrop

9slice-demo.skip: panels/buttons reference Unity sprite-registry addresses (UI/PanelFrame, UI/Sprites/*) that Chrome cannot load -- harness limitation, engine 9-slice is pinned by NineSliceSeamTests + the B-9SLICE-SNAP tracker

flex-playground._why: 5.63% baseline: text-heavy stress page, generic-family face split.

grid-playground._why: 5.20% baseline: same; includes the documented .as auto-placement Chrome quirk.

stats._why: 6.34% baseline since the s6.6 bare-fr floor (was 4.54): the gear-grid is the documented deferred aspect-ratio<->row-height case (SAMPLE_AUDIT) — the floor moves slot tracks TOWARD Chrome's 167px overflow without reaching it. Ratchet down when that case lands.
